items.headline: CAPE Launch Date Confirmed: CBP On Track for April 20 Deployment
items.summary: Court of International Trade confirmed CBP is making 'satisfactory progress' on CAPE system development, with all four components now 60-85% complete. CBP remains on track to launch Phase 1 by April 20, 2026, covering 63% of IEEPA-affected entries initially.

items.headline: Phase 1 Limitations Confirmed: Finally Liquidated Entries Deferred
items.summary: CBP's March 31 declaration confirms Phase 1 will exclude finally liquidated entries and complex scenarios like AD/CVD cases. These will be addressed in future phases with no timeline provided. Importers should continue filing protests to preserve rights.

items.headline: Electronic Refund Requirements: Only 26,664 of 330,000 Enrolled
items.summary: CBP data shows 92% of eligible importers lack required ACE/ACH enrollment for electronic refunds. Without proper setup, refunds will be rejected. Current enrollees represent 78% of entries by value ($120 billion) but small fraction of total importers.

items.headline: CIT Endorses CBP Progress, No Government Appeal Filed Yet
items.summary: Judge Eaton issued order praising CBP's 'satisfactory progress' on CAPE development. Government has 60-day window to appeal CIT's broad refund order but no appeal filed yet. Next CBP status report due April 14 with closed conference same day.

items.headline: 45-Day Processing Window Confirmed for Accepted Claims
items.summary: CBP committed to processing refund requests within 45 days of acceptance, unless compliance concerns require additional review. Refunds will be consolidated by liquidation date and importer, issued electronically only.

items.headline: CIT Expands Refund Scope to Include Finally Liquidated Entries
items.summary: Amended court order now requires CBP to reliquidate even finally liquidated entries 'without regard' to IEEPA duties. This significantly expands refund eligibility beyond the 90-day voluntary reliquidation window, though Phase 1 won't handle these cases.

items.headline: Interest Accrual Continues: $23M Daily Across All Eligible Importers
items.summary: Statutory interest continues accruing at approximately $650 million monthly across all IEEPA tariff payments. Total potential refund pool exceeds $170 billion with interest. Timing remains subject to court approval and potential government appeals.
